title: erythroxylum rignyanum
intro: Erythroxylum rignyanum, also known as the Bolivian coca, is a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae, native to the Andean regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Erythroxylum rignyanum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost energy levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be dried and made into tea, or used to create a tincture or infusion, although the use and regulation of this plant may vary depending on the region.
